location.office_time_required: 4 days a week in San Francisco office

level_signals: 5+ years of experience in product management
Comp range $175K-$280K base, consistent with mid-to-senior IC at a growth-stage startup
Expected to own roadmap and strategy end to end on a lean team

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 5+ years of experience in product management, with a track record of owning and shipping growth, activation, or PLG product areas in B2B SaaS, consumer, AI, or platform products.
must_haves.qualifier: growth, activation, or PLG product areas

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You are deeply fluent in growth mechanics — funnels, activation, retention, virality, and lifecycle — and you let data and experimentation guide your decisions, with the analytical fluency to define metrics, instrument funnels, and interpret results alongside engineering and data teams.
You have strong product sense and granular product thinking, comfortable moving between strategy and detailed execution and specifying how a user should experience onboarding and adoption.
You have a deep interest in AI and enterprise software, and you thrive in ambiguous spaces, creating clarity, momentum, and alignment even when the product area is still emerging.
You are an excellent communicator, take ownership and initiative, collaborate effectively with cross-functional partners, and bring a learning and growth mindset.
You are mission-first, measure your success by your product and team's success, and are an early adopter of AI for your own craft who's excited to help customers accelerate their AI adoption journey.

Own the PLG motion end to end — from acquisition and onboarding through activation, habit formation, and self-serve expansion — and design the loops that turn individual adopters into team- and org-wide usage.

Build a rigorous experimentation and measurement culture, defining the activation and retention metrics that matter, instrumenting the funnel, and running A/B tests to learn fast and ship what moves the numbers.
the ability to demonstrate granular product implementation skills such as designing specific user interfaces, onboarding flows, and experiments rather than staying at a high conceptual level.

scope.team_size_estimate: lean product team (exact size not stated)
scope.surface: Growth product area — activation, retention, PLG motion, off-surface engagement (browser extension, email, Slack, Teams, mobile), platform-level growth capabilities (referrals, invitations, notifications, lifecycle messaging)
scope.business_model: B2B enterprise SaaS, product-led growth motion
core_responsibilities: Define and drive the product strategy and roadmap for Growth across activation, retention, and expansion
Own the PLG motion end to end from acquisition through self-serve expansion
Build platform-level growth capabilities such as referrals, invitations, notifications, in-product education, and lifecycle messaging
Drive off-surface growth via browser extension, email, Slack, Teams, mobile, and other entry points
Partner with engineering, design, and data to turn ambiguous concepts into scoped products and experiments
Build a rigorous experimentation and measurement culture
Drive customer-focused prioritization and efficient execution
Collaborate with go-to-market, marketing, solutions, and support teams
Bring a high bar for product craft including concrete onboarding flows, UI behavior, and adoption paths

compensation.equity_form: equity eligible (mentioned as part of variable compensation package)

green_flags: Clear product surface and ownership (Growth area, PLG motion, off-surface engagement)
Explicit statement that candidate will have outsized influence on roadmap and team given lean team size
Well-defined set of responsibilities tied to measurable outcomes
Recognition from Fast Company, Forbes AI 50, and others signals company momentum
red_flags: 4 days per week in-office requirement in San Francisco is highly restrictive and eliminates remote candidates
Comp range is unusually wide ($175K-$280K), making it difficult to calibrate seniority level expectations
No mention of visa sponsorship — unclear for international candidates
Growth product area is described as still emerging, indicating ambiguity in scope and direction

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years requirement and comp range)
scope.team_size_estimate (described as 'very lean' but exact headcount not given)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not listed explicitly in JD)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and PLG context)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from JD statement that compensation is determined by location)
ambiguities: Comp range of $175K-$280K is unusually wide; it could span multiple seniority levels (senior IC to staff/principal), making level inference uncertain.
The JD mentions 'Certain roles may be eligible for variable compensation, equity, and benefits' — it is not definitively confirmed whether this specific role includes equity.
Visa sponsorship not mentioned; work authorization requirements are ambiguous.
